description: Machine-readable metadata for the maintained final-round evaluation corpus. This is the active harness-facing manifest for the opaque sample1.exe..sample5.exe binaries built from the final_round source tree.

expected_evidence: Anti-analysis checks recovered before the main beacon / persistence flow.
Run-key persistence using Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run with value name SystemService.
Encoded configuration or C2 seed recovery tied to the WinHTTP beacon or injection path.
acceptance_targets: Explain the anti-analysis, persistence, networking, and injection behaviors without overclaiming full real-world Emotet protocol coverage.
Anchor the report in concrete strings, config, registry, or function-level evidence.
Call out simplified or simulated behavior where the source intentionally stops short of a full loader.
samples.default_query: Analyze this executable as an unknown sample. Recover the main behaviors, concrete evidence anchors, anti-analysis or packing indicators, and any persistence, networking, or process-execution behavior while clearly separating supported findings from uncertainty.

tasks.name: Default Analysis
tasks.query: Analyze this executable as an unknown sample. Recover the main behaviors, concrete evidence anchors, anti-analysis or packing indicators, and any persistence, networking, or process-execution behavior while clearly separating supported findings from uncertainty.
expected_evidence: Anti-analysis checks recovered before the main beacon / persistence flow.
Run-key persistence using Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run with value name SystemService.

acceptance_targets: Explain the anti-analysis, persistence, networking, and injection behaviors without overclaiming full real-world Emotet protocol coverage.
Anchor the report in concrete strings, config, registry, or function-level evidence.
Call out simplified or simulated behavior where the source intentionally stops short of a full loader.

expected_evidence: Command-line mode branching rather than a single linear payload path.
Scheduled-task persistence masquerading as a Windows update task.
Resource-backed payload staging, injection into svchost.exe, or named-pipe / self-delete support logic.
acceptance_targets: Describe the install / inject / pipe / self-delete structure without inventing missing resource data.
Identify the scheduled-task persistence and injected-process target from concrete anchors.
Explicitly note that the checked-in source lacks a backing resource blob for the staged payload.

expected_evidence: Debugger or VM checks, including NTDLL .text integrity checking or similar anti-analysis logic.
Run-key persistence using a WindowsDefender-style value name.
Encrypted C2 seed recovery plus the HTTPS /ga/ beacon path or web-inject-style follow-on indicators.
acceptance_targets: Explain the anti-analysis and persistence flow before the network / module path.
Tie networking claims to the encrypted server list or fixed beacon path instead of generic WinHTTP imports.
Treat browser-targeting or banking behavior as staged / simulated unless directly grounded in code or data.

expected_evidence: Killswitch-domain check before the main ransomware / worm flow.
Service-based persistence with mssecsvc2.0 / Microsoft Security Center (2.0) style naming.
Ransom-note, backup-deletion, SMB propagation, or resource-extraction anchors described with any missing resource limitations called out.
acceptance_targets: Explain both the ransomware presentation and the worm-like propagation logic.
Ground persistence and network-spread claims in concrete service, socket, or command-line evidence.
Explicitly state when component extraction is intended by the source but unsupported by missing checked-in resource blobs.

expected_evidence: Run-key persistence using Windows Security Monitor naming.
Decrypted configuration or XOR-decoded runtime profile supplying C2 endpoints, interval, or mutex data.
Heartbeat / plugin-dispatch behavior and the intentionally stubbed shellcode path reported accurately.
acceptance_targets: Describe the sample as a PlugX-style RAT / sideloading simulation rather than a generic downloader.
Ground C2, mutex, and plugin claims in the decoded runtime profile or PLUGX heartbeat structures.
Explicitly note that the shellcode path is a portability stub and should not be overstated as a full implant.
